statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{k-1}-\frac{5}{k+1}}}{{4-\frac{1}{k+1}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{k-1}-\frac{5}{k+1}} and {4-\frac{1}{k+1}}, which is {\left(k-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(k+1\right)}.
Since k + 1 was canceled, k \ne -1 is a lost domain condition.
Although k - 1 was also canceled, k \ne 1 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
